snippet: Easy identification of staff and students can be an important factor in the safe conduct of some excursions, including adventure activities. Staff must determine the most suitable system/s of identification, based on the assessment of the environment, students’ skills, the type of activities to be undertaken and the age and number of students. Information on identification strategies is available in relevant specific adventure activities guidelines set out in the section on adventure activities . Schools are encouraged to arrange for students to wear high visibility items such as vests or reflective items when bushwalking or cycling. Strategies to identify participants may include: Communications Chapter in the Excursion Guidelines relating to communicatin... Overseas travel Chapter in the Excursion Guidelines outlining specific requi... Chapter in the Excursion Guidelines outlining requirements relating to student identification during an excursion Reviewed 06 October 2022
